notes: Task cards hardened: full handoff schema, boilerplate stripping, and low-signal auto-task guardrails in progress.

pomelli-dresses-refresh.title: Refresh Dresses product images (Pomelli photoshoot -> Shopify upload)
pomelli-dresses-refresh.summary: For each active product in Dresses collection, generate 4 Pomelli lifestyle images and upload them to the matching Shopify product with alt text and saved state.
blockers: Paused by human request until explicit resume command.
pomelli-dresses-refresh.next_action: Paused by human. Resume from second dress when told to continue.
pomelli-dresses-refresh.progress: 2/8 steps done (Pomelli UI validated, test upload worked).
pomelli-dresses-refresh.goal: Every active Dresses product has 4 new Pomelli images (flatlay, model try-on, in use, easter), uploaded to Shopify with descriptive alt text and a DONE/SKIP log line.

context.summary: Execution is product-by-product from top to bottom; do not start product B until product A is fully uploaded and saved.
context.why_exists: The worker agent runs in a fresh context window, so this card is the complete execution brief and source of truth.
constraints: Use currently open logged-in Chrome sessions.
Do not rename the downloaded original main image filename.
Use original filename as Shopify lookup key first, then product title fallback.
Do not save Shopify product until all 4 new images have alt text.
decisions: Pause requested by human until explicit resume command.
First product already handled manually by human; resume from second dress.
context.goal: Every active Dresses product has 4 new Pomelli images (flatlay, model try-on, in use, easter), uploaded to Shopify with descriptive alt text and a DONE/SKIP log line.

tools.how: Open collection, Pomelli, and Shopify tabs and process one product at a time.
Store 1 original image + 4 generated outputs per product; use date-modified sorting when ambiguous.
Upload 4 images, set alt text, reorder hero image, save and verify.

steps.text: A) Identify product and download original main image
procedure: Go to Dresses collection and open product page starting from top item, then continue top-to-bottom.
Download the primary product image to Downloads without renaming.
steps.definitionOfDone: One original main image is downloaded and filename captured for lookup.

steps.text: B) Generate 4 Pomelli outputs and download
procedure: Open Pomelli photoshoot and upload OriginalImageFilename image.
Select templates: flatlay, model try on, in use, easter.
Run generation, wait for render completion, download all 4 outputs.
steps.definitionOfDone: Exactly 4 new Pomelli images for the current product are in Downloads.

steps.text: C/D) Match Shopify listing, upload images, set alt text, save
procedure: Search Shopify by OriginalImageFilename first, then fallback to product title.
Confirm correct product and Active status; skip if Draft/Archived.
Upload 4 Pomelli images and set alt text format: [Full Product Title] — [template descriptor].
Save and confirm no unsaved changes indicator remains.
steps.definitionOfDone: Shopify product saved with 4 new images and complete alt text.

steps.text: Tracking and error-handling
procedure: After each product append DONE line: DONE | ProductTitle | OriginalImageFilename | PomelliOutputs=4 | ShopifySaved=Yes | Notes=...
If skipped append SKIP line with reason: NotActive / NotFound / PomelliError / UploadError / Other.
On Pomelli hang/fail: refresh once, retry once, then SKIP PomelliError and move on.
steps.definitionOfDone: Each processed product has one DONE/SKIP line for reliable resume and dedupe.

acceptanceCriteria: Every active Dresses product has 4 new Pomelli images uploaded to correct Shopify listing.
All 4 newly uploaded images per product have factual descriptive alt text.
Each product is saved in Shopify with no unsaved indicator.
DONE/SKIP tracking lines exist for each processed product to support resumability.

handoff.whatIsDone: Workflow/SOP captured; first dress already completed manually by human; automation permission setup partially validated.
handoff.nextAction: When resumed, start at second dress and execute steps A->D product-by-product.

CRON-20260318-consolidation.title: Memory Consolidation (3 AM)
CRON-20260318-consolidation.instructions: Follow knowledge/procedures/memory-consolidation.md EXACTLY. 1) Git safety net first. 2) Read today's memory file. 3) Extract events to ledger.jsonl. 4) Update knowledge files. 5) Check open loops. 6) Run integrity checks. 7) Regenerate recall/pack.md. 8) Git commit all changes. BEFORE marking done, fill in learnings with what was produced.

CRON-20260318-research-brief.title: Daily AI Research Brief (9 AM)
CRON-20260318-research-brief.instructions: 1) Open X (x.com/home) in clawd browser - read Following feed for AI news. 2) Search web for Clawdbot/Moltbot/Claude news (past 24h). 3) Check ClawdHub for new skills. 4) Identify Top 3 actionable improvements with sources. 5) Send research brief to Francisco on Telegram. BEFORE marking done, fill in learnings with discoveries and improvements found.

context.summary: Scheduled task from cron job 'research-brief'. Auto-created at scheduled time.
constraints: Must complete and log learnings before marking done

learnings.question: What did Daily AI Research Brief (9 AM) produce?

discoveries.item: Anthropic: 1M context GA for Opus 4.6 + Sonnet 4.6 (standard pricing; up to 600 images/PDF pages)
ClawHub positioning: skill dock with versioning/rollback + npx install snippet (useful clarity pattern)

top3_improvements.improvement: Use long-context mode deliberately when doing large audits/catalog reads (1M context GA).
top3_improvements.why: Fewer chunking failures; better cross-doc reasoning.

pinterest-strategy-research.title: Pinterest Marketing Strategy Research for DressLikeMommy
pinterest-strategy-research.description: Research and execute Pinterest marketing strategy for DressLikeMommy. Account has 848 followers, 10yr history, domain verified, but needs merchant verification, board restructuring, proper pin formats (2:3 vertical), varied descriptions, and 10-15 keyword-rich boards. Sub-agent completed audit and found 10 key issues.
pinterest-strategy-research.goal: DressLikeMommy Pinterest account fully optimized: verified merchant, 10-15 keyword-rich boards, proper 2:3 pin images, varied SEO descriptions, following niche accounts, video pins added.

steps.name: Research: Spawn sub-agent to audit current Pinterest state + best practices
Review sub-agent findings: audit saved to memory/global/dlm-pinterest-audit-2026-04-01.md
Apply for Pinterest Verified Merchant status
Restructure boards: fix misplaced products, create 10-15 keyword-rich boards
Fix pin image format: convert from 1:1 square to 2:3 vertical (1000x1500)
Rewrite pin descriptions: varied copy, keyword-rich, no repetitive openers
Follow 50-100 niche accounts for algorithm signals
Present full plan to Francisco for approval

decisions: Sub-agent ran on Sonnet to keep main session clean
Audit found 10 key issues — see audit_file

issues: Products on wrong boards (Christmas on Swimwear)

pinterest-board-reorganization.title: Pinterest Board Reorganization & Optimization
pinterest-board-reorganization.description: Execute Pinterest board reorganization: create keyword-rich boards, move misplaced pins, follow niche accounts, optimize for SEO.

steps.name: Move misplaced pins (Christmas→Christmas board, Valentine→Valentine board)
Check Easter board for misplaced pins
Follow 15-20 niche accounts for algorithm signals

context.blocker: Pinterest backend was rate-limiting pin moves after bulk board creation. Retry in ~30 min.
